scene1_hook: Every four seconds, somewhere in America, another payphone goes dark. But tonight, we're talking about Pittsburgh.
scene2_problem: Only three working payphones remain in the city. They survived the blackout of two thousand three. The Stanley Cup riots of oh nine. And the time your uncle used one to call your mom from a Primanti's.
scene3_stakes: Think about it. The last payphone that ever heard a breakup. The last one that dialed four one one because you forgot a number. The last one that ate your quarter and gave you nothing back. These phones have memories. Bad ones. But still.
scene4_solution: The Pittsburgh Last Payphone Preservation Society is on a mission. We polish the receivers. We replace the coin slots. We argue with Verizon. We do not answer the phone when it rings, because that would defeat the purpose.
scene5_ask: Become a Champion of the Dial Tone. For just nineteen dollars a month, you can help us keep three phones that nobody uses working, for reasons we can't fully explain. Visit savethephones dot pgh. Or don't. It's fine.
